100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 17, 1926, the British House of Commons debated the government’s handling of the ongoing coal strike, with tensions rising as miners continued to demand better wages and working conditions. In the United States, the Senate was divided over the proposed American Merchant Marine Act, aimed at bolstering the nation’s shipping industry. Meanwhile, in Germany, economic instability persisted as the country struggled to recover in the wake of World War I reparations.
